message:
feat(fr-command): MEMORY STATS upstream-shape Map reply (br-frankenredis-s14v)

Upstream object.c::memoryCommand returns MEMORY STATS as a RESP
map (RESP3) / alternating-key/value Array (RESP2 fallback) with
27 fixed fields plus a per-database sub-map. Our impl returned a
single BulkString of "key:value\\r\\n" lines — INFO-style format,
not a Map. Clients that try to decode the reply as a hash would
break.

Reshape MEMORY STATS to emit the upstream-shape Array with the
27 documented fields (peak.allocated, total.allocated,
startup.allocated, replication.backlog, clients.slaves,
clients.normal, cluster.links, aof.buffer, lua.caches,
functions.caches, db.0 sub-map, overhead.total, keys.count,
keys.bytes-per-key, dataset.bytes, dataset.percentage,
peak.percentage, allocator.allocated, allocator.active,
allocator.resident, allocator-fragmentation.ratio,
allocator-fragmentation.bytes, allocator-rss.ratio,
allocator-rss.bytes, rss-overhead.ratio, rss-overhead.bytes,
fragmentation, fragmentation.bytes).

Each value is Integer or BulkString-formatted-double matching
the upstream addReplyLongLong / addReplyDouble emission shape.
Counters we don't model (replication.backlog, clients.slaves,
allocator-fragmentation.bytes, etc.) emit 0/1.0 placeholders.

- fr-store::total_keys_across_dbs(): expose live key count for
  the keys.count field
- core_server.json: switch memory_stats fixture from any_bulk
  to any_array

fr-store 288 passed; fr-runtime 280 passed; fr-command 652
passed; fr-conformance 184 passed; cargo clippy --tests
-D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-s14v (MEMORY/LATENCY parity).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
